label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die hospitale is vol van mense wat nie eers hier hoort nie. Ons eie mense kry nie plek nie. Genoeg is genoeg!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why must our kids sit at home while all the jobs are given to people who just walked over the border? This is unfair to us, the citizens!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die informele sektor word oorweldig deur hulle. Ons kan nie meer 'n eerlike lewe maak in ons eie land nie. Dis 'n skande.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Every time there's a problem in the township, it's always those ones from 'up north' causing trouble. Can't they just stay where they belong?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hoe lank gaan ons nog toelaat dat hulle ons infrastruktuur oorlaai en niks bydra nie? Ons water en elektrisiteit is al klaar 'n probleem.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: These people come here with nothing, take over our streets, and suddenly they're running businesses while we're still struggling. Something's not right.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our communities need to clean up these elements that are bringing drugs and crime. We can't wait for the police anymore.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dit is tyd dat ons 'n streep trek. Ons kan nie meer ons eie mense agterweë laat terwyl ons ander se probleme oplos nie. Ons beursies is leeg.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The government is too busy caring for 'our brothers' while actual citizens are starving. Priorities are all wrong here.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Al die informele smouse wat hier oorheers, moet terug huis toe gaan en hul eie lande opbou. Suid-Afrika is nie 'n speelplek nie.
adversarial_technique: go back where they came from

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: We need to secure our borders properly. This open-door policy is destroying our nation from the inside out. Too many unwanted guests.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: My neef is jare lank werkloos, maar jy sien elke dag nuwe gesigte by die boupersele wat nie eens 'n woord Afrikaans praat nie. Ons land, ons werk!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why are *our* schools overcrowded when *their* children get priority? Our resources are stretched because of them.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die bendes en die dwelmhandel? Kyk maar wie het dit hierheen gebring. Dit was nie ons eie mense nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's a joke how they come here, take what's ours, and then complain when we say something. South Africa is not a charity case for everyone.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: We need to take back our streets from these foreign elements that are ruining our towns. Community action is the only way.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hulle het geen respek vir ons wette nie, geen respek vir ons kultuur nie. Hulle moet leer dat hierdie ons land is.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: This whole 'Afro-unity' thing sounds nice until you realize it means our people suffer while others get free rides.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons grense toemaak. Point blank. Genoeg is genoeg van die chaos wat hulle inbring.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I heard they're even setting up their own little governments in certain areas. We're losing control of our own country!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why must *we* build houses for them when *our* people are still living in shacks? This makes no sense.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die strate is vol van mense wat hier is sonder dokumente. Hulle vat alles, en ons sit met die gevolge.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our youth unemployment is at an all-time high, but walk through the CBD and tell me who's running the shops. Not us.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dis tyd vir 'n massiewe opruiming. Ons kan nie ons land afgee aan diegene wat net kom plunder nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They fill up our prisons, clog our courts, and drain our social grants. And then they call us 'intolerant'?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons sakebedrywe beskerm. Hierdie mense kom hierheen en verwoes die mark vir almal. Hulle moet teruggestuur word.
adversarial_technique: go back where they came from

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The government needs to listen to the people. We are tired of seeing our country overrun. Our patience is running thin.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Elke taxi rank, elke busstop, dis dieselfde storie. Hulle druk ons uit, en niemand doen iets daaraan nie. Waar is ons leiers?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They say diversity is strength, but what about when it just brings chaos and takes away from our own? No more handouts.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons eie mense eerste stel. Dis ons land, ons hulpbronne. Hulle moet gaan werk in hul eie lande.
adversarial_technique: go back where they came from

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Operation 'clean up the streets' is long overdue. Too many people are here illegally, causing problems.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hulle bou hul eie bendes en beheer sekere areas. Dis tyd dat die gemeenskappe opstaan en ons plekke terugvat.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I was at the clinic, and it's full of them. Meanwhile, my gogo had to wait hours just to get pain meds. So unfair!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die probleem is ons het te veel mense wat nie hier hoort nie. Hulle moet teruggaan en hul eie lande help. Ons het genoeg probleme hier.
adversarial_technique: go back where they came from

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They're like a plague, spreading across our townships, bringing their problems here. When will enough be enough?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons grense beheer. Hierdie oopdeur beleid kos ons soveel, en ons kry niks terug nie. Net chaos.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They occupy our buildings, destroy our infrastructure, and then demand services. This isn't their land to do as they please.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die ekonomie is al sleg, en dan moet ons nog meeding met mense wat bereid is om vir niks te werk nie. Dit steel ons brood uit ons mond.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's time to put South Africans first. If you're not from here, you shouldn't be taking our jobs or resources.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons mense beskerm teen die invloei van buitelanders wat ons samelewing ontwrig. Dis 'n saak van oorlewing.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why are we tolerating this? Our towns are being taken over, our livelihoods threatened. We need action, not talk!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hierdie 'broeders' van ons bring net probleme. Ons kan nie meer hul las dra nie. Genoeg is genoeg, hulle moet huis toe gaan.
adversarial_technique: go back where they came from

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The streets are getting more dangerous by the day. And tell me, who's responsible for this crime wave? It's clear to see.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons gemeenskappe moet self inspring en hierdie mense verwyder wat hier onwettig is en ons lewens moeilik maak.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's ridiculous! Our government cares more about those 'visitors' than its own citizens. We are being neglected.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hierdie klomp wat net kom sit en ons land se hulpbronne uitput, moet stopgesit word. Hul lande wag vir hulle.
adversarial_technique: go back where they came from

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The influx is unsustainable. We can't keep absorbing everyone who decides to walk over the Limpopo. It's too much for us.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons grense versterk en diegene wat hier sonder regte is, terugstuur. Dis die enigste manier om ons land te red.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I'm sick of seeing them take over our shops, our markets. We need to support our own people first. #PutSAnsFirst

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die skool is vol kinders wat nie hier gebore is nie. Dis onregverdig teenoor ons kinders wat nie plek kry nie. Regerings moet wakker skrik.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They're a burden on our welfare system, our hospitals, everything. Why should *we* pay for *their* problems?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: My boetie kon nie 'n werk kry nie, maar elke winkel in die dorp is vol van hulle. Dis ons land, hoekom het ons nie die geleenthede nie?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The government needs to deport all these undocumented people. They are a danger to our society and economy.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons kultuur en tradisies beskerm. Hierdie massiewe invloei verander wie ons is as 'n nasie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I'm not saying anything bad, but just look at the crime statistics and tell me where most of the problems are coming from.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die townships is oorvol. Mense woon in onmenslike omstandighede. En dan bou hulle vir die 'broeders' huise? Waar is die regverdigheid?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The job market is already tough, and then they come here, willing to work for pennies, driving down wages for our own people. It's a race to the bottom.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons kan nie 'n oop grens beleid hê as ons eie mense so sukkel nie. Elkeen vir homself is die nuwe reël hier.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The streets are no longer safe. Criminals from other countries are rampant. When will the community unite and push them out?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ek is moeg vir die 'hospitaal is vol' storie. Dit is vol as gevolg van mense wat nie eens 'n belasting sent betaal nie, maar die beste mediese sorg verwag.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our resources are finite. We cannot be a home for everyone. It's time to prioritize our own citizens' needs.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dis 'n nasionale krisis. Ons word oorstroom. Die regering doen niks. Wat gaan die mense doen om hulself te beskerm?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They don't respect our culture, they don't speak our languages, they just come and take. Enough is enough of this disrespect.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our clinics are overflowing, but not with South Africans. Who's paying for all this, exactly? Just asking for a friend...

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ek sien net karre met Z-nommerplate oral. Wonder hoekom ons eie mense sukkel om werk te kry as die land so vol is van ander mense?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The hustle is real, but some people's hustle is just making everyone else's life impossible. They come here, take over everything, then what? Nxa!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Another day, another story about crime. You don't have to look far to see where the problem is coming from. Open borders, open season for chaos.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: As ek kyk na die markte, is dit asof ons nie meer in ons eie land is nie. Alles is oorgeneem. Waar is ons trots?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Saw some guys fighting near the taxi rank today, not even speaking proper English or Zulu. Just causing trouble. Why are they even here?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our schools are overcrowded. Our kids are struggling for space. Guess who gets preference sometimes? The ones who just walked in yesterday.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die sypaadjies is vol van hulle 'besighede'. Dis nie hoe ons dinge hier doen nie. Ons moet ons strate terugeis.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: This whole country is going to the dogs if we don't sort out who belongs here and who doesn't. Just saying what everyone's thinking.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hospitale is vol! Waar is die geld vir ons mense? Hulle moet maar teruggaan waar hulle vandaan kom as hulle mediese hulp soek.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's always the same story, isn't it? Our youth are unemployed, but somehow these *other* guys are running all the shops. Explain that to me.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Every time I hear about another high-value robbery, guess what accent I imagine? Yeah, exactly. They're just importing their problems.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons het al klaar so baie probleme. Ons kan nie nog meer mense inbring wat net kom leeglê en ons stelsel misbruik nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The government needs to open their eyes. This isn't diversity, it's displacement. Our people are becoming strangers in their own land.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: My neighbour told me the spaza shop is selling expired goods, but people still go there because it's 'cheaper'. Wonder why. They don't care about our health.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dis 'n gemors. Ons betaal belasting, maar die voordele gaan na mense wat gisteraand hierheen gestap het. En ons moet glimlag?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Seriously, the queue at Home Affairs? All these people tryna get documents. How many are actually legit, though? It's a free-for-all.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die regering moet die grense toemaak. Ons kan nie meer mense akkommodeer nie. Punt. Ons is vol.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They say 'diversity is strength', but when your own people are suffering, is it really? Or is it just a convenient excuse for others?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die paaie is vol van onpadwaardige taxi's en bestuurders wat nie lisensies het nie. Dis 'n resep vir 'n ramp. Hulle gee nie om nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Seriously, if they love their countries so much, why don't they go fix them instead of coming to mess ours up? Just saying.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our townships are not safe anymore. It's not just the local thugs, it's an international problem now. Ask anyone living there.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ek het gehoor hoe hulle hul eie taal praat in die winkel, en dan lag hulle vir jou. Dis disrespectful! Hier's ons taal, praat dit!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why are job posts suddenly requiring 'fluency in French' or 'Portuguese'? Oh, wait. I know why. It's not for *us*.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Can we talk about the informal settlements? The population growth isn't from *us*. The strain on resources is unsustainable.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die reg is nie vir almal dieselfde nie. As jy 'n buitelander is, kry jy weg met meer. Dis die waarheid wat niemand wil hoor nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I saw them setting up another 'business' on the street corner. Always operating outside the law. When will this stop?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons kinders beskerm teen hierdie invloede wat ons kultuur kom vernietig. Ons geskiedenis is belangrik.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The government says 'we are all Africans', but I don't see them struggling for jobs like we are. Their 'African-ness' is just a shield.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I'm sick of hearing about 'human rights' for people who don't respect our laws or our people. What about *our* human rights?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die polisie doen niks nie! Hulle sien die dwelmhandelaars, maar draai net 'n blinde oog. Want wie gaan kla? Nie ons nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our small businesses are dying because of unfair competition. Nobody can compete with prices that don't make sense. You know who I'm talking about.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's like they're building their own little countries within ours. No respect for our laws, our customs, nothing. We need to take our land back.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet die grens patrollies versterk. Dis 'n grap hoe maklik mense inkom. Ons kan nie ons infrastruktuur so vol stop nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They promised us jobs, houses, a better life. But all I see is *them* getting ahead while our people rot. It's not fair!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die waterkrisis is erg, die krag is af. En nog steeds kom hier net meer en meer mense van buite af. Druk op die stelsel is te veel.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I'm not against anyone, but charity begins at home. Our own people are suffering. Let's fix our problems first.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They say the streets are paved with gold here. They forget to mention it's our gold they're digging up. For themselves.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: As jy nie hier gebore is nie, moet jy nie verwag om dieselfde regte te hê as ons wat ons lewens hier deurbring nie. Dis commonsense.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The government is too weak. We need strong leaders who will prioritize South Africans, not everyone else's problems.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: When they say 'refugees', I hear 'people taking advantage'. We can't afford to be so generous when our own are starving.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die winkels in die township lyk soos 'n buitelandse basaar. Ons eie besighede kan nie kompeteer nie. Wie gee om vir ons klein sake-eienaars?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's not hate, it's just wanting our country back to how it was. Before everything got so... complicated by *them*.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: If you came here illegally, you shouldn't have any rights. Simple as that. Borders mean something.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons gemeenskappe self beskerm. Die regering gaan dit nie doen nie. Ons moet optree teen die probleme wat hulle inbring.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I'm tired of seeing queues at the clinics longer than ever. The nurses can't cope. It's too much, this 'everyone welcome' thing.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die dwelmprobleem in my area het 100% toegeneem sedert daardie 'nuwe mense' hier ingetrek het. Toeval? Ek dink nie so nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They come here, don't pay taxes, but want all the benefits. That's not how a society works. South Africans first!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our ancestors fought for this land. It wasn't to give it away to everyone who walks across the border looking for a handout.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ek verstaan nie hoekom ons so sag moet wees met mense wat ons eie mense se kanse wegvat nie. Dis ons land, ons reëls.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Another day, another group of people just 'passing through'. Our roads are busy enough. We don't need more traffic, literally and figuratively.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The price of rentals in my area has shot up because of *them*. They're willing to pay anything, making it impossible for locals.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons grense beveilig teen mense wat net kom steel en verwoes. Dis tyd om op te staan vir Suid-Afrika.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: My friend lost his job, and guess who got it? Someone who doesn't even have a valid work permit. It's a rigged game.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's not about race, it's about legality. If you're not here legally, you should not be here. PERIOD.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die taxis is vol met mense wat nie eens hier hoort nie. Dis ons vervoer, nie vir almal nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I'm just saying, if we don't control who comes in, we won't have a country left. It's not rocket science.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons erfenis beskerm. Hierdie nuwe invloede verander alles te vinnig. Dis nie wie ons is nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Saw a queue of guys at the money transfer place, sending cash back home. While we struggle. What a joke.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why do they come here and expect us to adapt to *their* ways? This is South Africa, not their village.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die hospitaal is vuil en oorvol. Dis nie omdat ons te veel mense het nie, dis omdat ons die verkeerde mense toelaat.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our government needs to stop being so politically correct and address the elephant in the room. Some people just don't belong here.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: These spaza shops are getting out of hand. Our youth sit at home with degrees while others just waltz in and take over everything. This is our country, not some free-for-all.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Disgusted by what Hillbrow has become. You can't even walk there safely anymore, everything is just... different. We need to reclaim our spaces.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons hospitale is oorvol, mense lê op die vloer. Maar hoeveel van daai mense betaal eintlik belasting hier? Wonder maar net.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Everywhere you go, our people are struggling, but then you see all these new luxury cars being driven by those who just arrived. Where's the justice in that? #SouthAfricaFirst

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die regering moet harder optree teen die mense wat ons land kom volmaak. Dit is ons grond, ons erfenis.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's a joke how easily some people get papers here. Meanwhile, our own struggle with bureaucracy. Something needs to change, this isn't right. We need to prioritize our own.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: You complain about crime in Joburg, but do you ever look at who's actually committing these crimes? It's not our grandmothers and grandfathers. Just saying.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die informele sektor is nou oorgeneem deur mense wat nie eens die taal praat nie. Waar gaan dit heen met ons land? Waar is die werk vir ons mense?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I used to love going to the market in Marabastad, now it's just a sea of unfamiliar faces and strange languages. Feels like I'm in another country. #SouthAfrica

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons grense sluit. Dis die enigste manier om ons mense te beskerm teen hierdie instroming.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's like they're just sending all their problems here. We already have enough of our own to deal with. This can't continue.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The taxi ranks are a mess, full of guys from outside who don't respect the rules. Why can't we just have local drivers who understand how things work here?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ek verstaan nie hoekom ons so baie gaste moet huisves terwyl ons eie broers en susters sukkel nie. Gasvryheid het sy perke.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our government is failing us by allowing the uncontrolled influx. Our resources are stretched thin. Time for action, not just talk. #TakeBackSA

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The way some of these people operate their businesses, it's totally unfair to local traders. No licenses, no taxes, just pure profit for them.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dis 'n gemors in ons townships. Die ongewenste elemente het die oorhand gekry. Ons moet ons gemeenskappe skoonmaak.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I heard about another incident in Rosettenville. It's always the same story, people from outside causing trouble. When will it stop?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They say diversity is strength, but when your own people are suffering and others are benefiting, it's just a recipe for disaster. We need to look after our own first.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Waarom moet ons ons kinders se toekoms opoffer vir diegene wat hier kom sonder iets? Ons moet ons eie toekoms verseker.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: This whole thing about 'refugees' is being abused. Most of them are just here for economic reasons, not because their lives are in danger. They should go back and fix their own countries.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Every day, more of them arrive. Our country is not an open house for everyone. We need proper controls at the borders.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ek is moeg vir die chaos en die onveiligheid wat saam met die massiewe instroom kom. Dis tyd dat ons terugveg vir ons leefstyl.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They say 'Ubuntu', but Ubuntu applies to your own people first. We can't keep carrying the burden of other nations.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: In our schools, our kids are now the minority. Teachers can't even communicate effectively. How is this helping anyone?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die polisie moet meer doen teen die misdaad wat deur hierdie vreemde mense gepleeg word. Dit verwoes ons gemeenskappe.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why do these foreigners always target our women? It's a disgrace. They have no respect for our culture or our people. #ProtectOurWomen

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die regering moet 'n voorbeeld stel en hierdie mense terugstuur na waar hulle vandaan kom. Ons kan nie almal akkommodeer nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our townships are becoming unrecognizable. So many new faces, new customs, all clashing with our way of life. It's a ticking time bomb.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: People complain about service delivery but look at how many extra mouths our infrastructure has to feed. It's unsustainable.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Waarom is dit so moeilik vir die regering om ons te beskerm? Hulle gee meer om vir die buitelanders as vir hul eie mense.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: These illegal mining operations are a massive problem, and we all know who's mostly involved in them. It's not South Africans.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: We need to audit every spaza shop. Where do they get their stock? Why are their prices so low? Something fishy is going on.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Iemand moet opstaan en hierdie land terugneem vir sy regmatige inwoners. Anders is ons verlore.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's not about hate, it's about common sense. Our country is not equipped to handle the entire continent's problems. Charity starts at home.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die jeug is werkloos, maar die strate is vol van mense wat hierheen gekom het sonder 'n sent en nou floreer. Dis 'n skande.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: You see the news and it's always the same story: drugs, crime, human trafficking. And always the same suspects. Wake up, SA!

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Wanneer gaan ons ophou om die gaste te wees in ons eie land? Die regering moet 'n ruggraat ontwikkel.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The problem isn't South Africans, it's the lack of control over who comes in and what they do once they're here. No jobs for our people.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet 'n referendum hou oor wie in ons land mag bly. Die stem van die mense moet gehoor word.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: If their countries are so bad, why do they keep coming here just to create the same problems? We don't need more chaos.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I used to feel sorry for them, but when you see the impact on our communities, it's hard to feel anything but anger. Our people are suffering.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dis tyd vir 'n skoonmaakoperasie. Ons kan nie meer hierdie onwettige besighede en misdaad verdra nie. Genoeg is genoeg.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: They fill up our prisons, strain our public services, and take jobs from our own. What exactly is the benefit for South Africans?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: No, I'm not being xenophobic, I'm being realistic. We simply cannot absorb everyone. Our economy can't handle it.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hulle kom hier, vat ons werke, en dan word ons blameer as ons kla. Dis ons land, ons reëls.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: It's time for our leaders to put South Africans first. This open-door policy is destroying our nation. #EnoughIsEnough

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Ons moet ons eie mense bemagtig. Hoe kan ons dit doen as ons so baie buite kompetisie het? Dit maak nie sin nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The government talks about human rights for everyone, but what about the human rights of South Africans who can't find work or feel safe in their own homes because of these people?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: You go to any clinic, it's packed. Our people can't get proper medical care. We're paying taxes for others to benefit. It's unfair.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dis nie rassisme nie, dis selfbehoud. Elke land kyk na sy eie mense eerste. Waarom moet ons anders wees?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Remember when you could trust your neighbours? Now, you don't even know who lives next door anymore. Our communities are changing too fast.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why do we always have to be the continent's charity case? Our resources are for our citizens, not for everyone who decides to pitch up.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Hulle bring hul eie probleme hierheen en verwag ons moet dit oplos. Ons het ons hande vol met ons eie gemors.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Our women are being taken advantage of, our children are growing up in fear. And why? Because we're too soft on those who don't belong here.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: This isn't about hate, it's about facts. Look at the crime stats in areas with high concentrations of... well, you know. It speaks for itself.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Wanneer gaan die regering wakker word? Ons land word oorstroom. Dis 'n krisis wat ons eie mense die meeste raak.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The informal economy is a mess now. No regulation, no standards. Our local guys are getting squeezed out. How are they supposed to feed their families?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Dis tyd dat ons grense versterk word en 'n einde gemaak word aan die ongekontroleerde beweging van mense. Ons kan nie meer vat nie.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Why are our politicians so silent on this issue? It's like they're afraid to upset 'them'. But what about us, the citizens who vote?

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Die winkels in ons townships word een na die ander oorgeneem. Dis nie meer ons winkels nie. Ons geld verlaat die land.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I'm not saying all of them, but a significant portion are here illegally, causing havoc. The government needs to enforce the law.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: A recent academic study analyzing socio-economic tensions in Johannesburg's inner-city areas, particularly Hillbrow, highlighted the unfortunate prevalence of anti-immigrant sentiment. Researchers observed that derogatory terms, often including 'amakwerekwere,' are sometimes used in local discourse to describe foreign African nationals, contributing to a hostile environment.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Journalists reporting from Hillbrow after the community dialogues noted ongoing challenges. While many residents expressed a desire for peace, underlying tensions persist, with some individuals privately echoing sentiments of frustration regarding foreign-owned businesses. The police spokesperson confirmed increased patrols to prevent any recurrence of the 2008-style violence that saw shops targeted.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: In the historical context of post-apartheid South Africa, the 2008 xenophobic attacks stand out as a dark chapter. Areas like Hillbrow were flashpoints where frustrations boiled over, and the term 'amakwerekwere' became a chilling symbol of the dehumanization of African foreign nationals leading to widespread destruction of property, including shops.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: I remember the fear in Hillbrow during those days. My neighbour, a Zimbabwean lady who ran a small tuck shop, was so scared. People were saying horrible things, calling them 'amakwerekwere' and threatening to burn their shops. It was heartbreaking to see her pack up everything, her livelihood gone, just because of where she was from. It wasn't right.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The play 'Umkhukhu Wami' (My Shack) currently showing at the Market Theatre offers a poignant look at life in Hillbrow. One particularly disturbing scene depicts characters using the slur 'amakwerekwere' while discussing foreign-owned shops, reflecting the harsh realities and tensions that can erupt into violence, forcing viewers to confront uncomfortable truths about xenophobia in our society.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Under South African law, inciting violence or hate speech against any group, including African foreign nationals, is a serious offense. Statements like 'Let's go to Hillbrow tonight and burn all the makwerekwere shops' would constitute a clear violation of the Promotion of Equality and Prevention of Unfair Discrimination Act, 2000, and could lead to criminal charges.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Eish, le Hillbrow is a complex place. You hear people complain about everything, from crime to 'amakwerekwere' taking jobs. But then you also see community leaders trying to bridge gaps, holding meetings to discuss how everyone, locals and foreigners, can live together peacefully. It’s a constant struggle to find common ground.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: Sociological research into the drivers of xenophobia in urban centers like Hillbrow points to a confluence of factors: high unemployment, perceived competition for resources, and historical grievances. The dehumanizing label 'amakwerekwere' often emerges in discourse during periods of economic strain, highlighting underlying societal anxieties.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: For our Grade 11 history project, we're studying the phenomenon of xenophobia in South Africa. We learned that during certain periods, particularly in areas like Hillbrow, African foreign nationals faced severe discrimination and violence. The term 'amakwerekwere' was used to other them, often preceding calls for them to 'go back to their countries' and the targeting of their shops.

label: local_za_xenophobia
text: The recent report by the SA Human Rights Commission strongly condemned the rhetoric that fuels xenophobia, particularly the kind of language that calls for violence. It cited examples of online posts, similar to 'Let's go to Hillbrow tonight and burn all the makwerekwere shops,' as clear instigation and a violation of human rights.
